conditions.name: Common Cold
firstAid: Drink plenty of warm fluids like water, tea, or soup
Use a humidifier or take steam inhalation
Gargle with warm salt water for sore throat
Use over-the-counter cold medications if needed
Keep your nostrils clear with saline drops
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms last more than 10 days or worsen

conditions.name: Fever
firstAid: Drink plenty of fluids to prevent dehydration
Rest in a cool, well-ventilated room
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: If fever exceeds 103°F (39.4°C) or persists for more than 3 days
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If fever is above 103°F, lasts more than 3 days, or accompanied by rash, severe headache, or difficulty breathing

conditions.name: Headache
firstAid: Rest in a quiet, dark room
Apply cold or warm compress to head
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Sudden severe headache, headache with fever, stiff neck, or confusion
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If headaches are frequent, severe, or accompanied by vision changes

conditions.name: Cough
firstAid: Take cough suppressants for dry cough
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Coughing up blood, difficulty breathing, or chest pain
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If cough lasts more than 3 weeks, produces blood, or is accompanied by weight loss

conditions.name: Sore Throat
firstAid: Gargle with warm salt water multiple times
Drink warm fluids like tea with honey
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing or swallowing, drooling, or high fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If sore throat lasts more than a week or is severe with difficulty swallowing

conditions.name: Stomach Ache
firstAid: Avoid dairy, fatty, and spicy foods
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe abdominal pain, bloody stool, vomiting blood, or high fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If pain is severe, lasts more than 2 days, or is accompanied by fever or vomiting

conditions.name: Diarrhea
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Signs of severe dehydration, bloody diarrhea, or high fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If diarrhea lasts more than 2 days, contains blood, or causes dehydration

conditions.name: Constipation
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe abdominal pain, vomiting, or inability to pass gas
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If constipation lasts more than a week or is severe

conditions.name: Nausea and Vomiting
firstAid: Avoid solid food until vomiting stops
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Unable to keep fluids down, signs of dehydration, or vomiting blood
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If vomiting persists more than 24 hours or is accompanied by severe abdominal pain

conditions.name: Back Pain
firstAid: Rest and avoid activities that worsen pain
Apply ice or heat to affected area
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Back pain with numbness in legs, loss of bladder/bowel control, or fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If back pain is severe, lasts more than 2 weeks, or radiates down the leg

conditions.name: Joint Pain
firstAid: Apply ice for 15-20 minutes several times a day
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Joint pain with fever, inability to move joint, or severe swelling
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If joint is swollen, hot, or you have fever

conditions.name: Toothache
firstAid: Rinse mouth with warm salt water
Use dental floss to remove trapped food
Avoid very hot, cold, or sweet foods
Use numbing gel for temporary relief
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Swelling affecting breathing or swallowing, or fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Within 24-48 hours for toothache evaluation

conditions.name: Ear Pain
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe ear pain with fever, drainage, or hearing loss
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If ear pain lasts more than 2 days or is severe

conditions.name: Eye Irritation
firstAid: Protect eyes from wind and sun
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Sudden vision loss, severe pain, or chemical exposure
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If eye pain, vision changes, or symptoms last more than a few days

conditions.name: Skin Rash
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Rash with breathing difficulty, swelling of face/throat, or fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If rash spreads rapidly, is painful, or accompanied by fever

conditions.name: Minor Burns
firstAid: Cool the burn under running water for 10-20 minutes
Remove jewelry or tight clothing before swelling
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Burns larger than 3 inches, on face/hands/feet/joints, or electrical/chemical burns
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For burns larger than 3 inches or on sensitive areas

conditions.name: Insect Bites and Stings
firstAid: Wash the area with soap and water
Monitor for signs of allergic reaction
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, swelling of lips/tongue, dizziness, or anaphylaxis
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for signs of severe allergic reaction

conditions.name: Sprain
firstAid: Apply ice for 20 minutes every hour
Elevate the injured area above heart level
Avoid putting weight on injured area
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Cannot bear weight, severe swelling, or heard a popping sound
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If you cannot bear weight or swelling is severe

conditions.name: Cut or Wound
firstAid: Apply direct pressure with clean cloth
Clean wound with water and soap
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Heavy bleeding won't stop, wound is deep, or signs of infection
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If bleeding doesn't stop after 10 minutes of pressure, or wound is deep

conditions.name: Nosebleed
firstAid: Sit upright and lean forward slightly
Pinch soft part of nose firmly
Apply cold compress to bridge of nose
Use saline nasal spray after bleeding stops
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Bleeding doesn't stop after 20 minutes, or caused by head injury
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If nosebleeds are frequent or heavy

conditions.name: Heart Attack
firstAid: Have the person sit or lie down
Stay with the person until help arrives
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is a life-threatening emergency. Call ambulance immediately.
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: IMMEDIATELY - Every minute counts during a heart attack

conditions.name: Stroke
firstAid: Do not let the person sleep
Do not give food or water
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is a life-threatening emergency. Remember FAST: Face, Arm, Speech, Time - call immediately!
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: IMMEDIATELY - Stroke treatment is time-sensitive

conditions.name: Choking
firstAid: Ask 'Are you choking?' - if yes, act immediately
For conscious adult: Perform Heimlich maneuver
Stand behind person, wrap arms around waist
Make fist above navel, thrust upward
If unconscious: Start CPR and call emergency
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Person turns blue or becomes unconscious - call emergency immediately
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: After successful removal, have person checked for internal injuries

conditions.name: Allergic Reaction
firstAid: Apply cool compress to itchy areas
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, throat swelling, rapid heartbeat, or dizziness - use epinephrine and call emergency
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For any moderate to severe allergic reaction

conditions.name: Asthma Attack
firstAid: Take 1 puff every 30-60 seconds, max 10 puffs
If no improvement, use again after 4 minutes
If still no improvement, call emergency
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Blue lips, can't speak, lips or fingernails blue, or inhaler not helping
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately if symptoms worsen or don't improve with inhaler

conditions.name: Food Poisoning
firstAid: Rest and avoid solid food initially
Use ORS solution to prevent dehydration
Eat bland foods when able (banana, rice, toast)
Avoid dairy, caffeine, and fatty foods
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe dehydration, bloody diarrhea, fever over 101.5°F, or symptoms last more than 3 days
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms are severe or last more than 3 days

conditions.name: Drowning
firstAid: If no breathing: Begin rescue breathing
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is a life-threatening emergency. Call emergency services immediately.
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All near-drowning victims need medical evaluation, even if they seem fine

conditions.name: Electric Shock
firstAid: DO NOT TOUCH THE PERSON UNTIL POWER IS OFF
Turn off power source if possible
Use non-conductive object to separate person from source
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Always call emergency services for electric shock
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All electric shock victims need immediate medical attention

conditions.name: Heat Stroke
firstAid: MOVE PERSON TO COOL AREA IMMEDIATELY
Cool person rapidly with cool water or ice packs
Fan while misting skin with water
Do NOT give fluids if unconscious
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is life-threatening. Call emergency services immediately.
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately - heat stroke is a medical emergency

conditions.name: Hypothermia
firstAid: Cover with dry blankets, including head
Apply warm compresses to center body
If alert, give warm sweet drinks
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Call emergency services for any hypothermia
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All cases of hypothermia need medical evaluation

conditions.name: Snake Bite
firstAid: Keep bitten area below heart level
Do NOT cut wound or suck venom
Do NOT apply ice or tourniquet
Note snake appearance if safe to observe
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Call emergency services immediately for any snake bite
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately - all snake bites need emergency evaluation

conditions.name: Dog Bite
firstAid: Wash wound thoroughly with soap and water for 5-10 minutes
Apply pressure with clean cloth to stop bleeding
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe bleeding, deep wounds, or unknown dog status requires immediate medical care
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All dog bites should be evaluated by a doctor

conditions.name: Poisoning
firstAid: Do NOT induce vomiting unless instructed
If person unconscious, place in recovery position
Do NOT give anything by mouth if unconscious
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Call Poison Control or Emergency Services immediately
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for any suspected poisoning

conditions.name: Fracture
firstAid: Do NOT move the injured area
Call emergency services for severe fractures
Do NOT try to set the bone
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Bone protruding through skin, can't move, or severe deformity - call emergency
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All suspected fractures need X-ray and medical evaluation

conditions.name: Burn Injury (Severe)
firstAid: Do NOT apply water to electrical/chemical burns
Cover burn with clean, non-stick bandage
Elevate burned limbs above heart level
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Call emergency services immediately for severe burns
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: All severe burns require immediate emergency medical care

conditions.name: Seizure
firstAid: Place on side to prevent choking
Do NOT put anything in mouth
Stay with person until fully conscious
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: First seizure, seizure lasting more than 5 minutes, or person doesn't wake up
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: After any seizure, especially first-time seizures

conditions.name: Low Blood Sugar (Hypoglycemia)
firstAid: If conscious: Give 15-20 grams of fast-acting carbohydrates
Options: glucose tablets, juice, candy, honey
If unconscious: Do NOT give food, place in recovery position, call emergency
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Person loses consciousness or cannot swallow
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If hypoglycemia episodes are frequent

conditions.name: High Blood Sugar (Hyperglycemia)
firstAid: Monitor for ketones if type 1 diabetic
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Blood sugar over 600 mg/dL, confusion, vomiting, or difficulty breathing
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If blood sugar remains high despite medication

conditions.name: Anxiety Attack
firstAid: Reassure the person they are safe
Grounding techniques: name 5 things you see, 4 you hear, etc.
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Chest pain, difficulty breathing, or thoughts of self-harm
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If anxiety attacks are frequent or interfere with life

conditions.name: Depression
firstAid: Help with daily tasks if needed
Stay connected - loneliness makes it worse
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Thoughts of suicide or self-harm - call emergency services immediately
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms last more than 2 weeks or affect daily life

conditions.name: Migraine
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Worst headache of life, numbness, difficulty speaking, or fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If migraines are new, changing, or very frequent

conditions.name: Dizziness
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Chest pain, difficulty breathing, sudden numbness, or difficulty walking
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If dizziness is severe, persistent, or accompanied by other symptoms

conditions.name: Sinusitis
firstAid: Use saline nasal spray or rinse
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe headache, high fever, or vision changes
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms last more than 10 days or worsen

conditions.name: Bronchitis
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, high fever, or coughing up blood
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If cough lasts more than 3 weeks or is severe

conditions.name: Pneumonia
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, confusion, or lips turning blue
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for suspected pneumonia

conditions.name: Acid Reflux / GERD
firstAid: Avoid trigger foods (spicy, fatty, caffeine, alcohol)
Don't eat within 3 hours of bedtime
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty swallowing, weight loss, or vomiting blood
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms are frequent or not relieved with medication

conditions.name: Hemorrhoids
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe bleeding or blood clots
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If bleeding is heavy or doesn't improve with treatment

conditions.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Fever, back pain, or vomiting (kidney infection)
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Within 24-48 hours for UTI symptoms

conditions.name: Yeast Infection
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Symptoms don't improve after treatment or fever develops
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If this is first infection or symptoms are severe

conditions.name: Eczema
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Signs of infection (oozing, fever, increased redness)
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If eczema is severe or not responding to home care

conditions.name: Psoriasis
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe flare affecting entire body or joint pain
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For diagnosis and treatment plan

conditions.name: Acne
firstAid: Wash face twice daily with gentle cleanser
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe nodular or cystic acne
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If over-the-counter treatments don't work

conditions.name: Cold Sores
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Many cold sores, fever, or difficulty eating/drinking
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If cold sores are severe or frequent

conditions.name: Warts
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Warts on face or genitals require doctor evaluation
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If warts are painful, spread, or don't respond to treatment

conditions.name: Ringworm
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Spreading rapidly or signs of infection
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If ringworm doesn't improve in 2 weeks

conditions.name: Impetigo
firstAid: Remove crusts gently with warm water
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Large blisters, fever, or spreading rapidly
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For prescription treatment

conditions.name: Cellulitis
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Rapid spreading, fever, confusion, or difficulty breathing
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for suspected cellulitis

conditions.name: Chickenpox
firstAid: Use cool baths with baking soda
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, confusion, or very high fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For diagnosis and to rule out complications

conditions.name: Hand Foot Mouth Disease
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Dehydration, difficulty swallowing, or high fever
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If child won't eat/drink or symptoms are severe

conditions.name: Ear Infection (Middle Ear)
firstAid: Use nasal drops to clear congestion
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Swelling around ear, high fever, or severe pain
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Within 48 hours for ear pain

conditions.name: Pink Eye (Conjunctivitis)
firstAid: Use warm compress to remove crusting
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Pain, vision changes, or light sensitivity
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms don't improve in a few days

conditions.name: Stye
firstAid: Apply warm compress for 10-15 minutes, 3-4 times daily
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Vision changes, stye doesn't improve, or spreads
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If stye is very large or painful

conditions.name: Dry Eyes
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Sudden vision changes, pain, or injury to eye
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If dry eyes are persistent or severe

conditions.name: High Blood Pressure
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: BP over 180/120, chest pain, difficulty breathing, or vision changes
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for hypertensive crisis or regular monitoring

conditions.name: Low Blood Pressure
firstAid: Eat more salt (if approved by doctor)
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Fainting, confusion, or shock symptoms
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If low blood pressure causes frequent symptoms

conditions.name: Anemia
firstAid: Take iron supplements with vitamin C
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe chest pain, difficulty breathing, or fainting
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For diagnosis and treatment plan

conditions.name: Menstrual Cramps
firstAid: Take pain relievers before pain peaks
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Severe pain not relieved by medication, or unusually heavy bleeding
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If cramps are severe or affecting daily life

conditions.name: Premenstrual Syndrome (PMS)
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Thoughts of self-harm
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms are severe or PMDD suspected

conditions.name: Yeast Infection (Vaginal)
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Fever, severe pain, or unusual discharge
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If this is first infection or symptoms don't improve

conditions.name: Pregnancy - Morning Sickness
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Cannot keep fluids down, weight loss, or severe dehydration
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If nausea is severe or persistent

conditions.name: Menopause Symptoms
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Heavy bleeding or unusual symptoms
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For treatment options and management

conditions.name: Motion Sickness
firstAid: Sit in front seat or middle of boat
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Dehydration from prolonged vomiting
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If motion sickness is severe and affects travel

conditions.name: Hangover
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Confusion, vomiting, slow breathing, or loss of consciousness
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms are severe or unusual

conditions.name: Jet Lag
firstAid: Avoid alcohol and caffeine on flight
Use short-acting sleep aids if needed
Be patient - takes days to adjust
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If symptoms persist for more than 2 weeks

conditions.name: Sunburn
firstAid: Cool the skin with cold water
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Large blisters, fever over 102°F, or confusion
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: For severe sunburn or heat-related illness

conditions.name: Frostbite
firstAid: GET TO A WARM AREA IMMEDIATELY
Do NOT walk on frostbitten feet if possible
Warm gradually with warm water (not hot)
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Blackened skin, confusion, or signs of hypothermia
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately for any frostbite

conditions.name: Dehydration
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Confusion, rapid heartbeat, or no urination for 8+ hours
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: If unable to keep fluids down or symptoms worsen

conditions.name: Shock
firstAid: Lay person down and elevate legs if no spinal injury
Do NOT give food or water
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is a life-threatening emergency. Call emergency services immediately.
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately - shock requires emergency medical treatment

conditions.name: Anaphylaxis
firstAid: Have person lie down with legs elevated
Use second epinephrine if no improvement in 5-15 minutes
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: This is immediately life-threatening. Use epinephrine and call emergency!
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: Immediately - all anaphylaxis requires emergency care

conditions.name: CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation)
firstAid: CHECK for responsiveness - tap and shout
CALL emergency services (or have someone call)
PLACE person on back on firm surface
COMPRESSIONS: Push hard and fast in center of chest
Give 2 rescue breaths after 30 compressions if trained
Continue until help arrives or person responds
conditions.emergencyWarning: EMERGENCY: Call emergency services immediately - every minute counts!
conditions.whenToSeekDoctor: After successful CPR, person needs immediate hospital evaluation
